thesis: LB owns or manages a large, strategically located Delaware Basin surface portfolio and converts third-party activity into capital-light fees. Economic value should be anchored in normalized owner cash, with digital and power uses added only when enforceable net landowner economics are documented.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Contiguous surface ownership can create recurring capital-light access and use fees as third parties develop energy and infrastructure.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: A fee-activity bridge must reconcile normalized owner cash and maintenance capital across a cycle.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: Recent growth may be acquisition-driven or one-time rather than durable pricing power.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Recurring owner cash fails to reconcile or falls below the low-case path.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Digital infrastructure can create value only through LB's enforceable rent or participation net of obligations.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: An executed agreement must specify acreage, MW, rent/participation, commencement, termination, and capital obligations.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: Digital-infrastructure publicity may never convert to rent.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: No executed agreement or failure of the first contractual commencement condition.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Shareholder value is the OpCo economic claim after debt, NCI, tax distributions, and dilution.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: A per-share waterfall must reconcile OpCo cash to Class A and fully diluted claims.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: Enterprise growth may accrue partly to continuing OpCo holders or be financed by dilution.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Unreconciled ownership changes or financing claims exceed the modeled reserve.

open_questions: Obtain Alpha Digital's executed agreement and LB-attributable net economics.
Bridge fee growth into recurring volume, price, mix, acquisitions, and one-time activity.
Reconcile Up-C ownership, tax distributions, debt, and future unit issuance per share.
